summary: The post argues that senior engineers who want to advance their careers must stop waiting to be handed increasingly difficult problems and instead learn to identify problems themselves by examining their work in its 'next larger context.' Fournier explains that career progression beyond senior engineer rarely comes from solving harder assigned problems, but from finding where business opportunities intersect with technology solutions. She provides three detailed examples—a bespoke storage system, an inventory management system, and a developer tools team—showing how engineers can expand their perspective to find impactful work. The key is to look just one level broader than your current scope to discover adjacent opportunities, rather than waiting for projects to be assigned to you.
key_points: Career advancement past senior engineer rarely comes from being handed harder problems—it comes from identifying problems yourself
The key skill is finding where business opportunities intersect with what technology can solve
Looking at the 'next larger context' means understanding how your system serves users and fits into broader trends
Sometimes the advanced move is simplification or deprecation, not building something more complex
You should only look one context bigger to avoid overengineering—complex systems must evolve from simple ones
money_quotes: It is exceedingly rare that the path past senior engineer comes from being handed increasingly hard problems.
The blending of these two things, business opportunities and technology, is where you need to turn your eye, senior engineer.
Sometimes the advanced move is not to build a more complex thing, but to enable the company to embrace the broader trends.
You will only do that well if you understand why you need to differentiate and how to make the case for that based on the understanding of the larger context around your system, namely, its users.
Don't wait for the next project to come to you, seek it out by looking at the slightly bigger picture.
You look only one context bigger because it is easy to overdo things and violate our 'evolve complex systems from simple systems' principle when you try to go too broad.
key_insight: Senior engineers advance not by solving harder assigned problems, but by proactively identifying opportunities one context larger than their current scope—where business needs and technical possibilities intersect.
